name: Pima County Community Land Trust (Pcclt)
description_short: Arizona nonprofit community land trust offering permanently affordable homeownership through discounted home purchases, land lease model, and down payment assistance up to $33,000.
description_long:
Pima County Community Land Trust (PCCLT) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in 2010 in direct response to the 2008 housing crisis. The organization operates as a community-based land trust dedicated to addressing the affordable housing shortage in Pima County, Arizona. Since its inception, PCCLT has helped 123 families achieve homeownership through its innovative and permanently affordable housing model.

PCCLT offers multiple pathways to affordable homeownership and rental housing. The centerpiece is their Community Land Trust model, where the organization retains land ownership while buyers purchase the home itself at a discounted price. Homeowners pay a low monthly land lease with no down payment required, with additional subsidies keeping monthly mortgage payments affordable. The organization also provides HUD-approved housing counseling services, down payment assistance programs (WISH and HOME) offering up to $33,000 for eligible first-time homebuyers, inventory of 3- and 4-bedroom homes for purchase, and affordable rental properties. All programs serve individuals and families earning up to 80% of the area median income.

PCCLT distinguishes itself through its permanent affordability commitment—homes remain affordable for future generations, not just the initial buyer. The organization actively builds, rehabilitates, and stewwards properties while providing comprehensive support services. Their focus on community revitalization in the neighborhoods they serve, combined with ongoing counseling and financial assistance programs, creates a holistic approach beyond simple property transactions. The substantial down payment assistance (up to $33,000) removes a major barrier to homeownership for low-to-moderate income families.

As a nonprofit dependent on funding and donations, PCCLT's inventory and program availability may be limited compared to traditional lenders. The organization serves a specific geographic area (Pima County) and income-qualified buyers. While the model provides genuine long-term affordability benefits, the land lease component and retained land ownership may not appeal to all buyers seeking traditional fee-simple ownership. Program eligibility requirements and counseling participation are standard nonprofit practices but represent additional steps beyond conventional mortgage processes.
pros: Down payment assistance up to $33,000 through WISH and HOME programs removes major barrier to homeownership
Community Land Trust model retains permanent affordability for future generations, not just initial buyers
No down payment required—homeowners only purchase the house, not the land, reducing upfront costs
HUD-approved housing counseling services included to help families navigate homeownership
Serves low-to-moderate income families earning up to 80% of area median income
Established nonprofit with proven track record—123 families served since 2010 founding
Move-in ready homes available for purchase with active inventory of 3- and 4-bedroom properties
cons: Geographic limitation—serves only Pima County, Arizona; not available nationally or in other regions
Income qualification requirements restrict eligibility to families earning up to 80% AMI
Limited inventory compared to traditional real estate markets means fewer home options available
Monthly land lease payments continue indefinitely despite mortgage payoff, reducing true ownership equity
Dependent on nonprofit funding and donations, which may limit program expansion and responsiveness
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Pima County earning 50-80% of area median income seeking affordable entry into homeownership
Low-to-moderate income families wanting stability and equity-building but unable to afford traditional down payments
Renters seeking permanently affordable rental housing in community-revitalized neighborhoods
Homebuyers prioritizing long-term community stability over maximum equity appreciation
diagnosis: PCCLT is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers in Pima County, Arizona who need down payment assistance and prefer permanent affordability over traditional ownership. The main caveat is geographic restriction to Pima County and the ongoing monthly land lease obligation, which differs fundamentally from conventional mortgages where buyers eventually own both house and land.
services: Community Land Trust home purchases with discounted pricing
Land lease agreements with low monthly payments
Down payment assistance programs (WISH and HOME) up to $33,000
Property stewardship and ongoing homeowner services
Homes for sale inventory (3 and 4-bedroom properties)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
